messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] BioC2026 Schedule Posted; Registration Open for Conference and Post-Conference Activities
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] BioC2026 Schedule Posted; Registration Open for Conference and Post-Conference Activities
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor community,
Please see the following updates for BioC2026:

  *   The schedule<https://bioc2026.bioconductor.org/schedule/> is now available on the BioC2026 website.
  *   Registration is open for both the conference and the post-conference events<https://bioc2026.bioconductor.org/post-conference/> (Carpentries Workshop and Hackathon).

Please take a look at the schedule and register if you?re planning to attend. Early bird registration pricing ends on June 15th.
More details are available on the BioC2026 website<https://bioc2026.bioconductor.org/>.
Best regards,
Laurah Ondari
Bioconductor Community Engagement Support
Bioinformatician, International Institute of Tropical Agriculture (IITA)


	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] MSnbase timeout
messages.body_plain:
Hello - any idea or input regarding the recent MSnbase timeouts on
nebbiolo1 specifically during checking? On r-universe [0], there 
are
BiocParallel issues on Linux.

I can't reproduce neither locally nor on Github [1].

Laurent

[0] 
https://github.com/r-universe/bioc/actions/runs/25096610349/job/74234361041
[1] https://github.com/lgatto/MSnbase/actions/runs/25329303978

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] MSnbase timeout
messages.body_plain:
Hi Laurent,

I recently ran into the opposite problem of an inexplicable parallel test timeout with GitHub Actions that I was able to workaround by disabling testthat's parallel tests using the environmental variable TESTTHAT_PARALLEL=false (see https://github.com/ImmuSystems-Lab/toppgene/commit/b3ede460d6f106ddf32b131f2d194fc36bc15b1f); it may be worth trying out if one were to assume an underlying race condition or other stochasticity with testthat parallel tests. Not a great workaround, but, as you say, hard because to reproduce :/

Pariksheet

________________________________________
From: Bioc-devel <bioc-devel-bounces at r-project.org> on behalf of Laurent Gatto via Bioc-devel <bioc-devel at r-project.org>
Sent: Monday, May 4, 2026 1:03 PM
To: Bioc-Devel List
Subject: [Bioc-devel] MSnbase timeout


Hello - any idea or input regarding the recent MSnbase timeouts on
nebbiolo1 specifically during checking? On r-universe [0], there
are
BiocParallel issues on Linux.

I can't reproduce neither locally nor on Github [1].

Laurent

[0]
https://github.com/r-universe/bioc/actions/runs/25096610349/job/74234361041
[1] https://github.com/lgatto/MSnbase/actions/runs/25329303978

_______________________________________________

https://stat.ethz.ch/mailman/listinfo/bioc-devel

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] miRBaseVersions.db and miRNAmeConverter package maintainer
messages.body_plain:
Hi Bioconductor Team,

I hope you are keeping well. I am the package maintainer of the two
packages miRBaseVersions.db (annotation package) and miRNAmeConverter.

Since the last update I have swapped laptops at least three times and have
no idea anymore how to update the two packages. I read through the git
guidelines but I'm not quite sure.

Could you please help me set up SSH again and let me know how to push my
latest changes from my git repo to your server?

Thanks and kind regards,
Stefan

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Unreproducible build error with toppgene on devel branch on netbiolo2
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Unreproducible build error with toppgene on devel branch on netbiolo2
messages.body_plain:
I get a build error on devel [1] with R version 2026-04-17 r89917 [2] on netbiolo2 but I can't reproduce it locally with up-to-date packages and any of these development R versions:
2026-05-04 r89998
2026-04-20 r89917
2026-04-19 r89916
2026-04-19 r89915

Q1) netbioldo2 R r89917 is a bit old; how often is the devel branch R version updated?

Q2) Do failed package build automatically get re-run until they pass, in case their failures are dependency related?
Or is there a way to trigger a re-run without bumping the package version?
I ask because I wonder if it was a dependency version issue given the original error with several S4Vectors functions in the call trace:

* checking tests ...
  Running ?testthat.R?
 ERROR
Running the tests in ?tests/testthat.R? failed.
Last 13 lines of output:
    9.   ??methods::callNextMethod(y, ...)
   10.   ? ??base::eval(call, callEnv)
   11.   ?   ??base::eval(call, callEnv)
   12.   ??S4Vectors (local) .nextMethod(y, ...)
   13.     ??S4Vectors (local) .local(x, ...)
   14.       ??S4Vectors:::evalqForSubset(subset, x, ...)
   15.         ??S4Vectors:::evalArg(substitute(expr), envir, ..., where = parent.frame())
   16.           ??S4Vectors:::.find_arg_enclos(as.character(expr), where = where)
   17.             ??S4Vectors (local) matched_call(which)
   18.               ??base::match.call(...)
  
  [ FAIL 1 | WARN 0 | SKIP 0 | PASS 169 ]
  Error:
  ! Test failures.
  Execution halted

[1] https://bioconductor.org/checkResults/3.24/bioc-LATEST/toppgene/nebbiolo2-checksrc.html
[2] I'm not sure what's causing the 3 day mismatch in the date listed before the SVN revision.

Pariksheet
messages.body_snippet:
I get a build error on devel [1] with R version 2026-04-17 r89917 [2] on netbiolo2 but I can't reproduce it locally with up-to-date packages and any of these development R versions:
2026-05-04 r89998

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] miRBaseVersions.db and miRNAmeConverter package maintainer
messages.body_plain:
You should log into your BiocCredentials account

https://git.bioconductor.org/BiocCredentials/login/?next=/BiocCredentials/

You should add a ssh key there fore access; it will be applied to your Bioconductor account.

You will then need to push your changes to the devel (and maybe RELEASE_3_23 if bug correct) branches of Bioconductor with a valid version bump for it to propagate.

Let us know if you have any further questions

Cheers


Lori Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

________________________________
From: Bioc-devel <bioc-devel-bounces at r-project.org> on behalf of Stefan Haunsberger <stefan.haunsberger at gmail.com>
Sent: Monday, May 4, 2026 2:58 PM
To: bioc-devel at r-project.org <bioc-devel at r-project.org>
Subject: [Bioc-devel] miRBaseVersions.db and miRNAmeConverter package maintainer

Hi Bioconductor Team,

I hope you are keeping well. I am the package maintainer of the two
packages miRBaseVersions.db (annotation package) and miRNAmeConverter.

Since the last update I have swapped laptops at least three times and have
no idea anymore how to update the two packages. I read through the git
guidelines but I'm not quite sure.

Could you please help me set up SSH again and let me know how to push my
latest changes from my git repo to your server?

Thanks and kind regards,
Stefan

        [[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Unreproducible build error with toppgene on devel branch on netbiolo2
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Unreproducible build error with toppgene on devel branch on netbiolo2
messages.body_plain:
I'm not sure about the error but to answer your questions:

Q1)  The current cycle of release/devel, Bioc devel will also be on the release version of R (until the fall release when we will start using R-devel in prep for the Spring R release).  It should be the release candidate version of R 4.6 but we will likely update soon to the release version.

Q2) On our current system nebbiolo2, all packages are installed, built, and checked fresh daily.  So there will be a new run on today's report.


Cheers,


Lori Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

________________________________

Q2) Do failed package build automatically get re-run until they pass, in case their failures are dependency related?
